# Project Charter

## Project Title
Subsea Benthic Analysis AI – Multi-Modal Environmental Intelligence Pipeline

## Sponsor
NOAA / SUB / Petrobras

## Purpose
Develop an Artificial Intelligence pipeline for automated identification and
filtering of benthic-relevant subsea data to support environmental baseline
studies under SUB responsibility.

## Business Need
Subsea environmental datasets require extensive manual review. Automation is
required to reduce workload, increase consistency, and accelerate environmental
assessments.

## Objectives

### Primary Objective (MVP)
Develop a binary classification system to distinguish benthic-relevant from
non-benthic data.

### Secondary Objectives
- Scalable multi-modal pipeline
- AI-assisted annotation
- Foundation for future detection, segmentation, and taxonomy

## High-Level Scope

### In Scope
- Still image classification (MVP)
- Batch, offline processing
- AI-assisted annotation
- Human-in-the-loop validation

### Out of Scope
- Real-time deployment
- Full taxonomic classification
- Advanced multimodal fusion

## High-Level Success Criteria
- Precision ≥ 75% (benthic class)
- Dataset reduction ≥ 40–60%
- Retention ≥ 85–90% of true benthic data

## High-Level Risks
- No labeled dataset
- Annotation subjectivity
- Class imbalance
- Model underperformance

## Governance
Sponsor: NOAA / SUB  
Project Manager: Assigned by SUB  
Technical Authority: Data Science Lead  
Scientific Authority: Marine/Environmental Lead

## Approval
This Charter authorizes the project to proceed to Planning.


<!--
<br>

**Sponsor Signature:** ____________________
-->
